title: senecio haematophyllus
intro: Senecio haematophyllus, also known as New Zealand fireweed, is a plant species that belongs to the daisy family and is native to New Zealand. The plant contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which have been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Senecio haematophyllus include its potential to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as a natural remedy for skin conditions and wounds. Herbal preparations of Senecio haematophyllus can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health issues, but caution should be exercised due to potential toxicity.
